constraints: Exactly 3 steps in fixed order: personal info, document upload, selfie
Step indicator is positioned at the top of the component
Navigation between steps is via Back and Next controls
Document upload supports a file picker
Accepted document file types: PDF, JPG, PNG
Final step (selfie) displays verification status with exactly 3 possible values: pending review, approved, rejected

accessibility_gaps: No color pair for the upcoming/incomplete step indicator is specified; color.border.default (#D8DEE9) on color.background.default (#FFFFFF) is ~1.3:1 and color.border.strong (#A7B0C0) is ~2.5:1, both FAIL the 3:1 non-text/UI-component contrast requirement, so an outline-only 'upcoming' step marker would be indistinguishable from the background

Step indicator states (current/completed/upcoming) are not specified to carry a non-color signal; if distinguished by color alone (e.g., primary vs. border color) it fails WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Color) — step number, checkmark icon, and text label ('Step 2 of 3: Document Upload') must convey the state independent of color

Verification status values (pending review, approved, rejected) have no assigned token pairs; color.feedback.warning (#B54708 ~5.1:1) and color.feedback.success (#067647 ~5.4:1) and color.feedback.error (#D92D20 ~5.9:1) all pass 4.5:1 on white, but if placed on colored surface backgrounds (e.g., a tinted status card) contrast must be re-verified per background — only color.feedback.error.subtle (#FEF3F2) is defined in tokens for error; no corresponding subtle backgrounds exist for success/warning, risking an unvalidated custom background being introduced

The wizard has no role/landmark specified: multi-step forms require the whole wizard to be announced as a single form region (e.g., role="form" with aria-labelledby, or a <form> element) plus each step's heading in the accessibility tree so AT users know which step they are on after Back/Next

Step indicator lacks ARIA semantics: no aria-current="step" on the active step, and no group/list role/labels (e.g., ol/li with visually hidden 'Step 1 of 3, completed/current/upcoming' text) so screen reader users cannot determine progress

Back/Next navigation controls are not specified with disabled/loading state semantics: Next must be disabled (aria-disabled or disabled) until required fields on the current step are valid, and any async validation on Next must expose a busy state (aria-busy or Spinner with aria-label) so AT users aren't left in a silent wait

On step transition (Back/Next), focus is not specified to move; per SPA/wizard convention, focus must move to the new step's heading or first field so keyboard/AT users are not stranded on the removed Next/Back button

Document upload drag-and-drop has no keyboard-operable equivalent specified beyond 'supports a file picker' — the dropzone must be reachable via Tab and activatable with Enter/Space to open the file picker (native <input type=file> triggered by a labelled button, not a div with onClick only)

Upload dropzone lacks required ARIA: no aria-label describing accepted types/size ('Drag and drop or browse. PDF, JPG, PNG. Max 10MB.'), and drag-active state is not announced to screen readers (visual-only affordance)

File selection, upload progress, and file-type/size validation errors have no announcement strategy: rejecting a file over 10MB or wrong type must use role="alert" and be bound to the dropzone/input via aria-describedby with aria-invalid, per FormField pattern, not conveyed by color/icon alone
Selected file (upload-file-selected state) has no specified way to remove/replace it via keyboard — a focusable, labelled 'Remove file' control is required, not just a decorative icon
Selfie step (likely camera capture) has no fallback/labeling specified for camera permission denial or hardware absence, and no live region for capture status ('Photo captured', 'No camera detected')

Verification status panel (pending/approved/rejected) has no role specified: transient outcomes like 'approved'/'rejected' after submission should use role="status" (polite) for pending, and role="alert" (assertive) for rejected since it requires user action, per Alert component's role prop — brief does not select which

No focus-visible ring is specified for the step indicator segments, upload dropzone, file picker trigger, Back/Next buttons, or file-remove control; color.focus.ring (#1A5CFF, ~4.6:1 on white) must be applied as a ≥3:1, ≥2px visible ring on every one of these interactive elements

No prefers-reduced-motion handling specified for step-transition animations (e.g., slide between steps) or for any upload-progress/spinner animation; motion must be reduced/disabled under the OS-level preference

Async states (upload in progress, verification processing) have no live-region strategy specified: use role="status" (aria-live="polite") for progress updates and ensure the Spinner component's aria-label communicates what is loading (e.g., 'Uploading document', 'Verifying identity')

Personal info step form fields (implied by 'step-personal-info') are not enumerated with label/error bindings; each field must use FormField's htmlFor/label pairing plus aria-describedby for hint/error text and aria-invalid on validation failure, per CodeStyle.md defaults

color.text.disabled (#94A3B8 on white, ~2.7:1) fails 4.5:1 for body text; its use must be strictly confined to disabled control text (Button/Input disabled state), not to convey the 'upcoming' step label or any other readable status text

color.action.primary.disabled background (#A8C0FF) with color.text.on-action (#FFFFFF) is ~2.0:1, failing contrast, but is only acceptable for a genuinely disabled Next/submit button where no unique information is conveyed by that text alone — must be paired with disabled semantics (disabled or aria-disabled), not left as the only cue
responsive_gaps: below breakpoint.sm: step indicator must collapse to a compact/numeric form (e.g. 'Step 2 of 3') instead of showing all 3 labeled steps inline
below breakpoint.md: personal-info form fields stack vertically instead of any multi-column layout
below breakpoint.md: Back/Next navigation controls stack full-width and reorder so the primary action (Next) remains reachable by thumb without horizontal scrolling
below breakpoint.sm: the document-upload dropzone reflows to a full-width tap target sized for the file-picker fallback, since drag-and-drop is not a viable interaction on touch
recommendations: Add a `loading` state for document upload while the file is being read/validated, with a Spinner and disabled Next control until it resolves
Add a `loading` state after selfie submission while verification-pending-review is being determined, so users are never shown a blank gap between submit and result
Add a per-field `error` state on personal-info inputs bound to aria-describedby, driven by FormField's `error` prop, so invalid/missing required fields are surfaced before advancing
Add an `upload-error` state for document-upload distinguishing rejected file type (not PDF/JPG/PNG) and oversized file (>10MB), each with a distinct message rendered via Alert tone='error'
Add a `disabled` state for the Back control on step-personal-info (first step, nothing to go back to) and for the Next control until the current step's required fields/uploads are valid
Add a visible `focus` state (using color.focus.ring) for the step indicator items, the upload dropzone, and all form controls to satisfy keyboard navigation
Add an `empty` state for step-personal-info representing the pristine unfilled form, distinct from a filled-but-invalid state
Add a step-level `success` acknowledgment for document-upload and personal-info completion, separate from the terminal verification-approved state on the selfie step
Define layout behavior for the step indicator, upload dropzone, and Back/Next controls at breakpoint.sm and breakpoint.md so the wizard is usable on mobile
Add aria-current="step" to the active step indicator segment and use visually hidden text ('Step 2 of 3: Document Upload, current') so state is not color-only
Pair every step-indicator state (current/completed/upcoming) with an icon or number plus text, not color alone, and ensure completed uses color.feedback.success at ≥3:1 against its background
Wrap the whole wizard in a labelled <form>/region and move focus to the new step's heading on every Back/Next transition
Implement the dropzone as a labelled, keyboard-focusable control with a hidden native file input, Enter/Space triggers the picker, and aria-describedby lists accepted types and 10MB limit
Bind upload validation errors (wrong type, over 10MB) to the dropzone via aria-describedby + aria-invalid, and surface them with the Alert component using role="alert"
Use the Alert component with role="status" for 'pending review' and role="alert" for 'rejected' (and confirm 'approved' as role="status"), each with an icon plus text, not color alone
Add a labelled 'Remove file' button (Icon component with accessible name) next to the selected file so it is keyboard-operable
Apply color.focus.ring as a visible focus-visible outline (≥2px, ≥3:1) to step segments, dropzone, Back/Next buttons, and file-remove control
Wrap step-transition and upload/verification-progress animations in a prefers-reduced-motion media query to disable or shorten them
Use the Spinner component with an explicit aria-label ('Uploading document…', 'Verifying identity…') during async operations, inside a role="status" live region
Bind every personal-info field to FormField (label/htmlFor/error/hint) and set aria-invalid + aria-describedby on validation failure, per CodeStyle.md

issues_found.description: breakpoint.lg (1024px) is used in a media query but is not declared in spec.json's tokens array (only breakpoint.sm and breakpoint.md are listed there), even though it is used in spec.json's responsive[] behavior description and tokens.json does define breakpoint.lg. Recommend adding breakpoint.lg to spec.json's tokens list for full traceability, or documenting the responsive breakpoint choice explicitly against the design-system breakpoint table.

Several spec-declared tokens for 'disabled' and 'next-navigation' hover/active states are not present as var(--...) in the CSS module because that styling is delegated to the composed Button/Input components from @acme/ui. This is expected under a composition-based architecture and is not a compliance failure, but is noted so a reviewer does not mistake it for missing token coverage.

issues_found: Responsive media queries use hardcoded px literals (767px, 1024px) instead of referencing breakpoint.md (768px) / breakpoint.lg values; breakpoint.lg is not even declared in spec.json's tokens list (only breakpoint.sm and breakpoint.md are), so the 1024px large-screen query has no corresponding spec token at all. Accepted per task convention (breakpoints are not expressible as CSS custom properties in a media query), but flagged for spec/token alignment.

Spec tokens color.action.primary.disabled, color.text.disabled, and color.border.default (declared for the 'disabled' state) and color.action.primary.hover / color.action.primary.active (declared for 'next-navigation') are never referenced as var(--...) in KycVerificationWizard.module.css. This is not a hallucination or a raw literal, but expected coverage gaps because disabled/hover/active styling is delegated to the composed Button/Input catalog components rather than re-implemented in this module's CSS. No misuse of the tokens occurs; they are simply unused at this layer.

No 'subtle' success/warning background token exists in tokens.json (only color.feedback.error.subtle is defined); spec.json documents (open_questions) that verification-pending-review and verification-approved fall back to the neutral color.background.subtle for their Alert backgrounds instead of a tinted success/warning surface. This is a documented, intentional deviation carried from the frozen spec, not a hallucination introduced by the generator — Alert's actual background is owned by the @acme/ui component internals, not this CSS module.
